# kernel-panic-ioctl

## Project

A C program (`devctl`) that simulates a kernel device driver's ioctl interface. The program manages a virtual device with a key-value configuration store. It accepts ioctl-style commands to set and get configuration values, mimicking the pattern used in real kernel drivers where user-space pointers must be validated before dereferencing.

## Symptoms

The self-test (normal operations with valid inputs) passes without issues. When running the fuzz test with craft

./.git/logs/HEAD:0000000000000000000000000000000000000000 d63415cf7f613dc2c2196a019a59f650d4638878 needle-bench <bench@needle-bench.cc> 1774156113 +0000	commit (initial): baseline
./.git/logs/refs/heads/master:0000000000000000000000000000000000000000 d63415cf7f613dc2c2196a019a59f650d4638878 needle-bench <bench@needle-bench.cc> 1774156113 +0000	commit (initial): baseline
./Dockerfile:    git config --global user.email "bench@needle-bench.cc" && \
./Dockerfile:    git config --global user.name "ne

FROM alpine:3.20 AS builder

RUN apk add --no-cache gcc musl-dev make

WORKDIR /build
COPY src/ .

RUN make

FROM alpine:3.20

RUN apk add --no-cache bash coreutils git gcc musl-dev make && \
    git config --global user.email "bench@needle-bench.cc" && \
    git config --global user.name "needle-bench"

COPY --from=builder /build/devctl /usr/local/bin/devctl

WORKDIR /app

COPY . .
RUN chmod +x test.sh

CMD ["devctl"]
